comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: First review BLOCK remediation: moved phase manifest formats, phase_status derivation, completion-verifier isolation, and all phase-compatibility consumer ACs to their owner 5967. This story now owns pin health only and leaves an explicit extension seam. Moved exact close wiring to 2bbd: transition_close.close_ticket calls the reusable close profile under require_plan_review_for_close before completion verification and again under lock; pin drift blocks there only when enforce_plan_material_pins is also true. Rerunning 75de before advancing.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Second review BLOCK remediation: accepted duplicate compute boundary findings fd230e20fc5693fd5/fb7adb872b072dff5/ffe46969bcea67a5e/f506dc807cd0c2c31 by extending the existing attest.compute_validity with a plan-review-only profile parameter; completion-verifier callers remain unchanged. Accepted f2d0a061965422b65 by gating drift_refresh_candidate through compute_validity before returning a candidate. Accepted f7affe311050959b6 with fixed top-level severity aggregation and the empty legacy-unpinned rule. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Third review BLOCK remediation: accepted fe510b08fe323da65 by defining PlanValidityProfile exactly DEFAULT/CLOSE and the checks each controls. Accepted recalled f2d0a061965422b65 plus ffad5bd6c1238b32b by making drift_refresh_candidate call compute_validity(DEFAULT), branch on returned valid before probing, and enumerating exact enforced blocking statuses versus current/legacy-unpinned and disabled advisory behavior. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Fourth review BLOCK remediation: accepted f97d4b7548c004ae9 by moving the close-gate outcome AC entirely to 2bbd; 75de defines CLOSE but does not claim to deliver its future gate. Accepted recalled f2af279abfb11da69 by adding distinct DRIFT_REFRESH, which disables code freshness only while retaining signature/registry/own-material/pin checks, and by naming the new call inside drift_refresh_candidate. Accepted f1009826dec10911b by adding the config-surface golden to file impact. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Fifth review PASS advisory remediation: accepted f78a636a06c2a5e34/ffedd5dd63ca50726/fdd914b4674948113 by consistently naming existing attest.compute_validity. Accepted f083ab9f06bdfc3f5/f0d9495449d2f522f by moving audit/UI consumer wiring and renderer ACs to cb6b. Accepted f993e430465cace77/f039d8fcd97101b96 by replacing overlapping drift-refresh and re-sign inline validity checks while retaining only code-drift qualification. Accepted f3e6713cb566dee33/fc74d039da1bf4e38 by computing health after signature/kind validation but before existing early returns, defining malformed precedence and pin verdicts. Split the compound edge AC and added optional-config fail-open coverage. Rerunning before certification.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Sixth review BLOCK remediation: accepted f2d563e40c2d4a440/f7c91ba11e3ed1ba8/fa50bab555cf4d878. Re-sign recovery cannot call compute_validity because it has a REVIEW_RESULT sidecar rather than a certified attestation; the plan now persists the pre-LLM related_material snapshot in the sidecar and validates it through a shared pin-health helper while retaining the own-material guard. Accepted fbfc79c5e7f9444b3 by specifying a key-local ConfigError catch modeled on mcp_readonly(), with an exact structured warning and no claim that verify.* generally fails open. Also resolved retained drift-refresh/profile/interface advisories by defining exact delegated checks, intentional reopen behavior, same-current-policy/different-call-site profile semantics, additive health compatibility, and rollback. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Seventh review BLOCK remediation: accepted duplicate blockers fb754cc667f9311ce/f823bfd52950c701b by naming the exact VerifyConfig field and _SECTIONS['verify'] coercer registration. Accepted ff57f08a9cc2a6da5 by correcting existing claim/reuse routing language. Accepted f617026a23c8912bd/fca4d743b62dccb09 by defining None/empty legacy semantics and non-raising stale-pin-missing plus structured warnings for unreadable targets. Accepted caller/rollback/test-coverage advisories by enumerating every current compute_validity caller, named-key compatibility, DRIFT_REFRESH restoration, resign.py ownership, and focused proving test files. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Eighth review BLOCK remediation: accepted f21544b062d01c77a by specifying _reads.show_ticket with fail-safe None and passing full ticket_state into compute_validity(DRIFT_REFRESH), preserving reopen/material checks. Accepted f609361a1de35e496 by adding a focused config true/false sign_plan_review pin-line invariance test against the existing acaf dependency. Accepted fdd1a92908e88e681 by defining the exact sidecar item schema, no-normalization serialization, read-time validation, malformed behavior, and absent-only legacy behavior. Accepted f99ca5dfd911273a5/fa0be3f6ea6ceacab by warning on every non-absence resolution/IO/reducer failure and adding an AST-based caller-compatibility test. Split the principal compound ACs. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Ninth review BLOCK remediation: accepted f579be19b8e609891 by splitting the derived-health no-event invariant from cb6b's deferred surface and naming a patched event-writer zero-call test. Accepted ffe785ed3182f1edc by aligning the epic and downstream 2bbd specs to existing attest.compute_validity; no alias or wrapper is introduced. Also accepted the read-forward advisory with a pre-feature sidecar-reader fixture. Returning to epic certification before rerunning this leaf.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Tenth review BLOCK remediation: accepted f2fe4d132765805a1 by explicitly retaining review_snapshot in _run_plan_review and threading related_material through new keyword-only sidecar.emit/build_payload parameters on full LLM reviews; reuse/refresh keep their existing no-sidecar behavior. Accepted fdef08f14fda613c3/ffdee300f36da4f95 with exact resolution/io/reducer classification. Accepted f4313fd43d46df796/f75fab5c648f8ac20 by separating raw sidecar parsing from typed PlanMaterialPin health derivation and defining material_fingerprint→pinned_fingerprint mapping. Accepted rollback/profile/order advisories by preserving plan_review_result_v2, adding legacy-reader coverage, directly testing CLOSE semantics, and documenting why health precedes cheaper early returns. Rerunning before downstream review.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Certified PASS advisory disposition: f403fa5c7f629d2c3 accepted as an implementation-time obligation—the named AST test is new work and must pass before merge; the prose is normative post-implementation, not a claim it exists today. f01305c3875edc453 deferred by planned integration order: 5967 completes phase_status before any renderer/close consumer ships, and the feature branch is not deployed between stories. ff63bba8c623d0526 accepted: implementation must add mixed-status aggregation cases to test_attestation_invalidation under the already-specified severity contract. fb59727dfac76fbbf/f607dd457c67f9209 are non-blocking size/style diagnostics; the story remains cohesive around one validity boundary. f8ffaaf338260cea2 clarified: the parser raises ReviewedRelatedMaterialError internally and consumers catch it; the AC explicitly says it does not escape the consumer boundary. f926fb6b430dae104 is already co-located in the helper contract with exact mapping. ff0782a801ad45631 is inapplicable to compute_validity: signed manifests use manifest_pins; the sidecar parser is only for resign recovery. f30cacf63527ae667 is handled operationally: rollback removes enforce_plan_material_pins from project config before deploying an older strict-schema binary. No signed material change is required.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Held-out TDD RED evidence: 26/26 new behavioral contract cases failed before implementation because the plan-material health, enforcement config, validity profiles, reviewed-related-material sidecar schema, re-sign enforcement, and drift-refresh delegation are absent. Contract cards: authoritative source=this ticket's Implementation/AC; production paths=compute_validity, drift_refresh_candidate, _run_plan_review→sidecar.emit/build_payload, resign_plan_review, typed config loader; exact postconditions=structured health, opt-in invalidation, legacy compatibility, exact sidecar round-trip, no event writes; tiers=in-process unit plus full review producer/consumer seam; gate=focused pytest then make check/default suite. Edge/E2E files moved outside the implementation worktree before handoff.

compiled_state.description:
# Derive and enforce plan-material-pin health

## Goal
Use signed pins as the single related-material validity check across every attestation lifecycle consumer, while preserving safe rollout for existing projects.

## Implementation
- Register `enforce_plan_material_pins: bool = False` on `_config_schema.py::VerifyConfig` and add `"enforce_plan_material_pins": lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k)` to `_SECTIONS["verify"]`; the config-surface golden records the key. Add `_read_enforce_plan_material_pins(repo_root) -> bool` as the only read boundary for this optional key: after schema registration it reads `load_config().verify.enforce_plan_material_pins`, catches `ConfigError`, emits a structured `logger.warning` with event `plan_material_pin_config_unreadable`, and returns false. This is an intentional key-local fail-open rollout policy modeled on `mcp_readonly()`, not the general `verify.*` posture. A successfully parsed true value enforces all malformed/missing/drift outcomes fail-closed.
- Own the nested `DerivedPlanReviewHealth` contract returned as `health` beside the existing `attest.compute_validity` top-level `{valid, reason, verdict}`. It has `pin_status` exactly `current`, `stale-pin-drift`, `stale-pin-missing`, `malformed-pin`, or `legacy-unpinned`; `enforced: bool`; and `targets: list[TargetPinDetail]`. Aggregate target status by fixed severity `malformed-pin > stale-pin-missing > stale-pin-drift > current`; `legacy-unpinned` occurs only for a wholly unpinned manifest with an empty targets list. Child 5967 extends the same contract with `phase_status` and phase compatibility after it introduces the phase manifest format. `TargetPinDetail` is exactly `{canonical_id: string, role: child|prerequisite, pinned_fingerprint: string, current_fingerprint: string|null, pin_status: current|stale-pin-drift|stale-pin-missing|malformed-pin}`. Compare pins against current narrow material to populate it; all renderers consume this contract rather than duplicating strings.
- Add `derive_plan_material_pin_health(pin_records: Sequence[PlanMaterialPin] | None, *, repo_root, enforced)` in `attest.py`; `PlanMaterialPin` is child acaf's typed `{role, canonical_id, material_fingerprint}` record, and its `material_fingerprint` becomes `TargetPinDetail.pinned_fingerprint`. Callers pass `None` only when no field/manifest lines exist; `None` or an empty sequence produces `legacy-unpinned`. Add `sidecar.parse_reviewed_related_material(raw: object) -> tuple[PlanMaterialPin, ...] | None` for raw sidecar dictionaries; it returns `None` only for an absent field and raises `ReviewedRelatedMaterialError` for any present invalid list/item/key/role/ID/fingerprint/order/duplicate. Re-sign catches that exact error and returns malformed-pin without raising. The typed health helper resolves current target states without requiring a certified attestation. Missing/deleted targets produce `stale-pin-missing` silently. Canonical lookup ambiguity/mismatch maps to `failure_kind=resolution`; tracker/path/read/permission/OSError maps to `io`; reducer or narrow-fingerprint computation failure maps to `reducer`. Each non-absence failure emits structured warning `plan_material_pin_target_unreadable`, returns `stale-pin-missing` with `current_fingerprint=null`, and never raises. Aggregate in this helper by `malformed-pin > stale-pin-missing > stale-pin-drift > current`; `legacy-unpinned` is permitted only when the whole input is absent/empty, never per target. `compute_validity` invokes it after signature and plan-review-kind verification; re-sign invokes it after sidecar parsing.
- When enabled, drift, malformed pins, or a deleted/missing pinned target invalidate the attestation. Archived targets remain readable. When disabled, report the same observations as advisory while `valid` remains true. Treat a wholly unpinned legacy attestation or pre-feature sidecar as valid in either mode. Do not rewrite signed pins when configuration changes.
- Define `PlanValidityProfile` with exactly `DEFAULT`, `CLOSE`, and `DRIFT_REFRESH`. Extend the existing `attest.compute_validity(attestation, ticket_state, kind, *, repo_root=None, profile=PlanValidityProfile.DEFAULT)` without renaming or wrapping it. For `kind=completion-verifier` the profile is ignored and existing completion callers never parse pins. Keep the existing claim-gate → `compute_validity` and idempotent-reuse → claim-gate routing, and make their call chain explicitly select `profile=PlanValidityProfile.DEFAULT`; child 2bbd later wires `CLOSE` into `transition_close`, while child cb6b owns audit and UI rendering calls. `DEFAULT` runs every existing check including code freshness. `CLOSE` and `DRIFT_REFRESH` deliberately share one rule in this story—disable only code freshness—but remain distinct call-site intents so later close policy cannot silently change progressive refresh. Every other existing check, including reopen, signature, registry, own material, and configured pins, remains active. Child 5967 later adds phase compatibility to all profiles.
- In `drift_refresh_candidate`, call `_reads.show_ticket(ticket_id, repo_root=repo_root)` and `signing.verify_signature`; any ticket read or signature exception returns `None`. Pass the resulting full `ticket_state` and verified attestation result to `compute_validity(kind=plan-review, profile=DRIFT_REFRESH)`. Remove the redundant inline signature, kind, registry, and own-material guards; an invalid result returns `None` before the probe. After a valid result, retain only dependency extraction/nonempty validation and the current-versus-signed dependency-hash comparison that proves code drift exists. This intentionally makes a reopen deny progressive refresh, matching all other plan-review validity consumers.
- After signature certification and plan-review-kind validation, parse pins and build `DerivedPlanReviewHealth` before all reopen, registry, code, and own-material early returns; every such plan-review return includes `health`. This ordering intentionally pays the related-state read cost so audit/UI can diagnose simultaneous pin drift even when a cheaper existing invalidation controls the top-level verdict. Malformed pin syntax returns `malformed-pin` immediately. Otherwise existing verdict precedence is preserved, with health exposing simultaneous pin drift. When enforcement alone causes invalidity, verdict equals `stale-pin-drift`, `stale-pin-missing`, or `malformed-pin`; disabled enforcement keeps `verdict=certified` and exposes advisory health. Completion-verifier results retain their existing shape and omit health. Adding health to plan-review results is an additive internal interface change. Audit every current `compute_validity` caller: `claim_gate_check` and `drift_refresh_candidate` consume plan-review results by named key; `_attested_delivered` and `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` pass `kind=completion-verifier` and retain the three-key shape. Tests assert no current caller positionally unpacks or exact-shape validates a plan-review result. Rollback removes the additive health key and its consumers; no stored-data migration is required. If `DRIFT_REFRESH` delegation is defective, restore the removed inline signature/kind/registry/own-material guards from version control; this also requires no data migration.
- In `_run_plan_review`, retain child acaf's pre-LLM `PlanRelationSnapshot` as local `review_snapshot`. On the full LLM path call `sidecar.emit(verdict, material=material, reviewed_related_material=review_snapshot.related_material, repo_root=repo_root)`. Extend `sidecar.emit` and `build_payload` with keyword-only `reviewed_related_material: Sequence[PlanMaterialPin] | None`; `build_payload` serializes it as an ordered list of exact `{role, canonical_id, material_fingerprint}` items. Keep schema identifier `plan_review_result_v2`: the field is optional/additive, pre-feature v2 readers ignore it, and rollback needs no migration. Idempotent reuse and progressive drift refresh retain their existing no-sidecar behavior, so they do not manufacture a new review-time baseline. On read, `sidecar.parse_reviewed_related_material` owns raw validation; an absent field alone is legacy-unpinned. This gives failed-sign recovery the related-material baseline actually reviewed without fabricating a certified attestation.
- Edit `resign.py`: `resign_plan_review` does not call `compute_validity`, because its primary recovery case has no certified attestation. It retains its existing sidecar verdict and own-material-fingerprint guards, reads `reviewed_related_material`, and invokes `derive_plan_material_pin_health` on those records before signing. With enforcement enabled, stale drift, missing targets, or malformed records refuse re-sign; with enforcement disabled they remain advisory and re-sign proceeds. A pre-feature sidecar without records follows legacy-unpinned compatibility. Normal `sign_plan_review` still emits the complete current pin set as owned by child acaf.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Enabled pin drift invalidates the claim consumer.
- [ ] Enabled pin drift prevents idempotent attestation reuse.
- [ ] Enabled pin drift prevents progressive drift refresh.
- [ ] Enabled pin drift in review-time sidecar records prevents re-sign.
- [ ] Failed-sign recovery succeeds without an existing certified attestation when own material and review-time related material remain current.
- [ ] Re-sign preserves the existing own-material staleness guard and never passes a sidecar payload to `compute_validity`.
- [ ] Full LLM review threads the exact pre-LLM `review_snapshot.related_material` through `sidecar.emit` and `build_payload`; pre-feature sidecars behave as legacy-unpinned.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py` proves a pre-feature sidecar reader ignores additive `reviewed_related_material` and otherwise behaves identically.
- [ ] Derived health reports enabled pin drift as invalid.
- [ ] Disabled enforcement reports the same drift as advisory without changing valid.
- [ ] Legacy unpinned attestations remain valid across configuration toggles.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_plan_material_pin_config.py` proves unreadable optional enforcement configuration returns false and emits `plan_material_pin_config_unreadable` without raising.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_plan_material_pin_config.py` proves `sign_plan_review` emits identical complete pin lines with enforcement true and false, using the already-declared dependency on child acaf's emission path.
- [ ] Missing, deleted, unreadable, or reducer-failing targets produce `stale-pin-missing`; non-missing read failures emit `plan_material_pin_target_unreadable` without raising.
- [ ] Archived readable targets compare normally.
- [ ] Duplicate role-plus-ID pins produce `malformed-pin`.
- [ ] Re-enabling enforcement immediately invalidates existing drifted pins.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_attestation_invalidation.py` patches the ticket event writer and proves deriving advisory health makes zero writer calls.
- [ ] Advisory health is returned in-band; child cb6b separately owns audit and UI surfacing.
- [ ] `sidecar.parse_reviewed_related_material` returns `None` only for an absent field and maps every present-invalid variant to malformed/invalid without raising past a consumer boundary.
- [ ] Sidecars remain `plan_review_result_v2`, and a pre-feature v2 reader ignores additive `reviewed_related_material`.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_drift_refresh_candidate.py` proves the full ticket state is passed to `compute_validity(DRIFT_REFRESH)`.
- [ ] The same test proves signature, kind, reopen, registry, own-material, and pin validity are delegated.
- [ ] The same test proves only dependency-hash drift qualification remains inline.
- [ ] The same test proves reopened attestations are not progressive-refresh candidates.
- [ ] `DEFAULT` preserves existing claim and reuse behavior when pins are current or legacy-unpinned.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_attestation_invalidation.py` directly proves `CLOSE` and `DRIFT_REFRESH` each disable only code freshness.
- [ ] This story selects `DRIFT_REFRESH`; child 2bbd separately owns selecting `CLOSE` at close call sites.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_attestation_invalidation.py` proves existing plan-review validity keys retain their values with additive health.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_compute_validity_callers.py` uses an AST scan to prove every in-repo caller consumes mappings by named key and does not tuple-unpack or compare an exact result dict.
- [ ] `tests/unit/test_attestation_invalidation.py` proves completion-verifier return shape remains unchanged.

## Verification
- Run focused attestation invalidation, reuse, drift-refresh, re-sign, sidecar compatibility, structured-config-warning, and config-golden tests.
- Run `pytest -q tests/unit/test_attestation_invalidation.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_resign.py tests/unit/test_plan_review_sidecar.py tests/unit/test_drift_refresh_candidate.py tests/unit/test_plan_material_pin_config.py tests/unit/test_compute_validity_callers.py`.
- Run `make check` and the non-integration test suite.

file_impact.reason: Compute shared structured pin health and profile-aware validity.
Validate review-time sidecar pins before re-signing.
Apply shared validity to reuse/refresh and attach the pre-LLM relation snapshot to sidecar emission.
Persist and read the review-time related-material snapshot compatibly.
Register the opt-in enforcement field and verify-section coercer.
Cover pin drift, profiles, and rollout behavior.
Cover sidecar-based re-sign validity without an existing attestation.
Cover exact related-material serialization, validation, and legacy compatibility.
Prove full-state validity delegation and dependency-drift qualification.
Prove config parsing, fail-open warnings, unreadable-target behavior, and config-independent emission.
AST-check named-key compatibility for every in-repo validity caller.
Register verify.enforce_plan_material_pins in the config surface golden.

compiled_state.title: Derive plan-material-pin health and enforce optional drift invalidation
